subject: Statistics
title: Confounding Variables in Health Research
description: A fast breakdown of Confounding Variables in Health Research for builders.

hook.text: Coffee drinkers have higher cancer rates. But coffee drinkers also smoke more. Which causes the cancer?

buildup.text: A confounder is a hidden variable that affects both the exposure and the outcome, creating a false link.

discovery.text: Smoking is the confounder. It's linked to both coffee drinking and cancer, creating a spurious association.

twist.text: You can never be sure you've found all confounders. Observational studies always have this shadow of doubt.

climax.text: Randomized trials eliminate confounding by design. Observational studies try to control for it statistically.

punchline.text: The real cause hides behind the visible one. That's confounding.

quiz.question: Why are confounders dangerous in health research?
options: They create false associations between an exposure and an outcome by affecting both
They make the sample size too small
They only appear in randomized trials
